mcqs.question: What is the primary difference between a DFA and an NFA?
mcqs.explanation: A DFA is deterministic, meaning that the next state is uniquely determined by the current state and input symbol. An NFA is nondeterministic, meaning that the next state is not uniquely determined.

mcqs.question: Which type of automaton is more powerful?
mcqs.explanation: An NFA is more powerful than a DFA because it can recognize a wider range of languages.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the transition function in a Finite Automaton?
mcqs.explanation: The transition function determines the next state based on the current state and input symbol.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of a language that can be recognized by a DFA?

options.text: The set of all strings of binary digits that end with a 1
The set of all strings of binary digits that contain at least one 1
The set of all strings of binary digits that contain exactly one 1
The set of all strings of binary digits that contain no 1s

mcqs.explanation: The language of all strings of binary digits that end with a 1 can be recognized by a DFA.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of a language that cannot be recognized by a DFA?

options.text: The set of all strings of binary digits that end with a 1
The set of all strings of binary digits that contain at least one 1
The set of all strings of binary digits that contain exactly one 1
The set of all strings of binary digits that contain no 1s

mcqs.explanation: The language of all strings of binary digits that contain at least one 1 cannot be recognized by a DFA.
